content.full_text:
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
428 J Street • Suite 620 • Sacramento, CA 95814-2329
(916) 322-5660 • Fax (916) 322-0886

July 1, 2005

Patrick Faverty, Dr.
Superintendent
Rio School District
3300 Cortez Street
Oxnard, CA 93036-1309

Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. W-05-135

Dear Dr. Faverty:

This letter confirms the receipt of your June 28, 2005, request for advice regarding the
City of Oxnard, Water Department, Superintendent. Under the provisions of California Code of
Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B), unless the Superintendent has authorized you as his or her
representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide
analysis. I have enclosed a copy of regulation 18329 for your review.

If you believe a violation of the Political Reform Act has occurred, you may file the
enclosed complaint form with our Enforcement Division.

Should you have any additional questions regarding this matter, please contact the
Commission at (916) 322-5660.

Sincerely,

Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel

By: John W. Wallace
Assistant Counsel, Legal Division

Enclosure
WJL:rd

sections.question_synthetic: Dr. Faverty requested advice regarding the City of Oxnard Water Department Superintendent under California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B).
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC declines to provide advice because Dr. Faverty does not appear to be an authorized representative of the Superintendent under the applicable regulation.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: This is a declination letter (W-05-135) with no formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections; the decline rationale is embedded in the body text. OCR formatting issues present in original (e.g., 'Farn PolrrrcAI-').

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Dr. Faverty requested advice regarding the City of Oxnard Water Department Superintendent under California Code of Regulations section 18329(b)(8)(B).

CONCLUSION: Unless the Superintendent has authorized you as his or her representative, the Fair Political Practices Commission cannot address your questions or provide analysis.

embedding.summary: The FPPC declines Dr. Faverty's June 28, 2005 request for advice regarding the City of Oxnard Water Department Superintendent, stating that without authorization from the Superintendent, the Commission cannot provide analysis.
